findings.title: test-update-check.sh crashes silently — grep pattern + set -e abort
evidence: scripts/test/test-update-check.sh:73 — output=$(...update.sh --check...) runs expensive real git operation
scripts/test/test-update-check.sh:76 — '\\|' in basic grep is GNU extension, may abort with set -e on grep error (exit 2)
run-phase0.sh output: test exits non-zero, output truncated after '--- --check output sections ---'
findings.why: With 'set -euo pipefail', a grep exit 2 (syntax error on BSD grep for \\|) causes immediate script termination. The update.sh --check invocation on line 73 is also a real expensive operation (git fetch, checksums) that runs TWICE (line 45 and 73) — not hermetic, not fast.
findings.source: SWE@Google Ch.11 — tests must be hermetic; Google Testing Blog flaky-tests series
findings.fix_sketch: 1. Replace \\| with grep -E or two separate grep calls. 2. Mock update.sh --check instead of running real git. 3. Verify all grep patterns work on both GNU and BSD grep. 4. Add explicit handling for grep exit 2.

findings.title: test-manifest-files.sh legitimately fails — root version (0.28.0) != CHANGELOG (0.28.1)
evidence: scripts/test/test-manifest-files.sh — version consistency check fails: root version (0.28.0) != CHANGELOG (0.28.1)
findings.why: Real version skew. MANIFEST.yaml hasn't been bumped to match CHANGELOG.md. This is a legitimate test failure that signals a real project issue — either MANIFEST is stale or CHANGELOG was prematurely bumped.
findings.source: SWE@Google Ch.12, Principle 1 — tests should be reliable indicators; false failures erode trust
findings.fix_sketch: Bump MANIFEST.yaml version to 0.28.1, or revert CHANGELOG if 0.28.1 not yet released. Run test-manifest-files.sh to verify.

findings.title: 9 production scripts completely untested — no bash -n, no ShellCheck, no behavioral tests
findings.why: These 9 scripts total 542 lines of production code with ZERO test coverage. The 7 hooks are critical path — they gate AI session lifecycle (OPZ protocol, workspace resolution, artifact validation). A bug in any hook silently corrupts the AI's work context. This is the Beyoncé Rule violation: if you rely on it, you should have tested it.
findings.source: SWE@Google Ch.11 — Beyoncé Rule: any production behavior the team relies on must be guarded by an automated test
findings.fix_sketch: Phase 1: Add bash -n + ShellCheck for all 9 scripts (quick win). Phase 2: Add unit tests for hooks that gate session state (protocol-stop-gate.sh, protocol-artifact-validate.sh). Phase 3: Add integration tests for workspace resolution (resolve-workspace.sh).

findings.title: No test coverage for ai-cli-wrapper.sh — central AI abstraction layer
evidence: scripts/ai-cli-wrapper.sh — provides Claude Code → OpenCode fallback. Central dependency for all AI operations.
Grep in scripts/test/ shows only passive references (path variable), no behavioral tests.
Previous audit finding C4 (broken --allowedTools flag) was found manually, not by test — proving the gap.
findings.why: ai-cli-wrapper.sh is the single abstraction over all LLM calls. A bug here (wrong flag format, broken fallback logic) affects every AI test (eval-day-open.sh, eval-strategy-session.sh) and all production AI interactions. The previous C4 bug (literal quote chars in --allowedTools) went undetected — showing the test gap is real and dangerous.
findings.source: SWE@Google Ch.11 — Beyoncé Rule; Khorikov — test the boundaries that real callers use
findings.fix_sketch: Add test-ai-cli-wrapper.sh with: bash -n, flag construction tests (no embedded quotes), fallback logic tests (mock claude unavailable), tools parsing tests.

findings.title: run-phase0.sh and run-e2e.sh lack set -euo pipefail — can silently skip failures
findings.why: Without 'set -e' (errexit), a failing command inside the test loop doesn't halt the runner. If a test script has a silent failure in the middle, the runner continues without detecting it. Combined with the grep filter that truncates output, this can produce false-PASS results.
findings.source: SWE@Google Ch.12 — tests must be reliable; Google Bash Style Guide recommends set -euo pipefail for all scripts
findings.fix_sketch: Add 'set -e' to both runners. Verify all existing tests still pass with stricter mode. Consider adding 'set -e' to all test scripts that currently lack it.

findings.title: 10 test files missing set -euo pipefail — inconsistent strictness
findings.why: Inconsistent strictness: 18 files use set -euo pipefail, 10 don't. E2E tests are especially dangerous without -e because they run multi-step integration workflows where a mid-step failure silently continues, producing false confidence.
findings.source: Google Shell Style Guide; SWE@Google Ch.11 — hermetic tests must detect failures reliably
findings.fix_sketch: Add 'set -euo pipefail' to all test files. Run full suite after change to catch newly exposed failures. Fix any tests that relied on lax mode.

findings.title: seed-day-open.sh has 11 || true — potential error masking in AI seed pipeline
findings.why: 11 instances of '|| true' in a 483-line seed script. Several are on critical git operations (push, commit, init) — if any fail, the AI smoke test runs on incomplete or wrong seed data, producing false PASS/FAIL on the LLM judge evaluation. The '|| true' pattern on git push means a network failure during seed setup won't be detected.
findings.source: Auto-retry as Flake Mitigation antipattern (Google Testing Blog); SWE@Google Ch.11 — tests must report failures accurately
findings.fix_sketch: Replace '|| true' with explicit error handling: capture rc, log, decide whether to abort or skip. For git operations that MUST succeed (push, commit), remove || true entirely. For optional operations (gh issue create), check preconditions before attempting.

findings.title: No trap cleanup in 16 of 28 test files — temp file leaks can cause flakiness
evidence: 16 test files use mktemp without trap cleanup
scripts/test/test-update-apply.sh — creates temp dirs, no trap
scripts/test/test-update-apply-mock.sh — creates temp dirs, no trap
findings.why: Without trap EXIT, temp directories created by mktemp persist after test completion. Over time, /tmp fills with stale test directories. On systems with limited /tmp space, this causes flaky failures when mktemp can't create new directories.
findings.source: Shared Mutable State antipattern (Luo et al. 2014; Romano et al. ICSE 2021)
findings.fix_sketch: Add 'trap "rm -rf \"\$TMPDIR\"" EXIT' after every mktemp call. For tests with multiple temp dirs, consolidate into a single cleanup trap.

findings.title: test-phases.sh: grep -q && _ok without || _fail — M1 from previous audit still unfixed
evidence: scripts/vm/test-phases.sh:56 — echo '$output' | grep -q 'Template source files' && _ok 'validate: template section' \
scripts/vm/test-phases.sh:58 — echo '$output' | grep -q 'Workspace runtime' && _ok 'validate: workspace section' \
findings.why: The 'grep -q && _ok' pattern without '|| _fail' means if the expected text is missing, the test silently produces no assertion at all — neither pass nor fail. The counter isn't incremented. This was identified in the 2026-05-06 audit (M1) but remains unfixed.
findings.source: Assertion Roulette antipattern (Meszaros); SWE@Google Ch.12 Principle 9 — clear failure messages
findings.fix_sketch: Add '|| _fail "validate: <section> missing"' to both lines. Verify all assertions are accounted for in the final count.

findings.title: test-update-check.sh runs real update.sh --check — expensive, slow, not hermetic
evidence: scripts/test/test-update-check.sh:45 — output=$(...update.sh --check...) runs real git fetch + checksum verification
update.sh --check performs: git fetch upstream, checksum generation (touches checksums.yaml), extension compatibility checks
findings.why: The test runs the REAL update.sh --check twice, which performs git operations, regenerates checksums.yaml (a side effect!), and checks extension compatibility. This is: a) slow (multiple seconds), b) not hermetic (modifies checksums.yaml on disk), c) flaky (depends on network for git fetch). A unit test for --check should mock the upstream or test individual functions.
findings.source: SWE@Google Ch.11 — hermeticy; Sleeps as Synchronization antipattern (Google Testing Blog)
findings.fix_sketch: 1. Extract --check logic into testable functions. 2. Mock git operations in test. 3. Test output sections against canned output, not live run. 4. Don't modify real checksums.yaml during test.

findings.title: test-manifest-files.sh: YAML validity skipped — no ruby, and python3 YAML validation not attempted
evidence: scripts/test/test-manifest-files.sh — output shows '- skipped YAML validation (no ruby)'
python3 is available on the system but not used for YAML validation in this test
findings.why: test-manifest-files.sh checks YAML validity but only tries ruby, not python3. Python3 is available (test-checksums.sh:137 uses it for YAML validation). This means an invalid MANIFEST.yaml would pass this check silently if ruby is missing.
findings.source: SWE@Google Ch.12 Principle 9 — clear failure messages; structural testing should validate what it claims
findings.fix_sketch: Add python3 YAML validation fallback (same pattern as test-checksums.sh:137-146). Report 'skipped' only if neither ruby nor python3 is available.

findings.title: No ShellCheck in local test suite — only bash -n syntax check
evidence: ShellCheck exists in CI (validate-template.yml) but is not installed or invoked locally
All test-*.sh files use bash -n for syntax checking, never shellcheck
ShellCheck would catch: unquoted variable expansions, SC2086, SC2155, unused variables
findings.why: bash -n only checks syntax (parse errors). ShellCheck catches semantic issues: unquoted expansions (potential word splitting bugs), unused variable assignments, incorrect test operators. Running only bash -n leaves a whole class of bash bugs undetected in local testing.
findings.source: Testing Trophy (Dodds) — static analysis is the base layer; ShellCheck is the ESLint of bash
findings.fix_sketch: 1. Add shellcheck invocation to run-phase0.sh (skip if not installed, warn). 2. Add shellcheck severity=warning scan for all .sh files in pre-commit hook (already in CI). 3. Fix existing ShellCheck warnings.

findings.title: run-phase0.sh grep filter truncates assertion output — may hide failures
evidence: Filter hides: intermediate output, debug messages, errors that don't contain grep patterns
findings.why: The grep filter on line 37 aggressively truncates test output, only showing lines with pass/fail markers. In debug mode (--verbose), the full log is shown. But in normal mode, failures that produce error messages without '✗' prefix are hidden — the user only sees the final '✗ FAIL: testname' without context.
findings.source: SWE@Google Ch.12 Principle 9 — clear failure messages must survive the CI→developer pipeline
findings.fix_sketch: 1. Always show the FULL output on test failure (already done on line 45, but only when the grep filter is reached). 2. Consider always showing output for failed tests.

findings.title: e2e/_lib.sh — shared E2E library has no tests
evidence: scripts/test/e2e/_lib.sh — shared library sourced by all 5 E2E tests
No test file validates _lib.sh functions independently
findings.why: All 5 E2E tests depend on _lib.sh for shared logic. A bug in _lib.sh would affect all E2E tests, producing correlated failures that are hard to diagnose. Testing test infrastructure is important — it's production code for your test suite.
findings.source: SWE@Google Ch.12 Principle 3 — test your test infrastructure; Kapelonis antipatterns catalog
findings.fix_sketch: Add test-e2e-lib.sh that validates _lib.sh functions in isolation: path resolution, temp dir creation, assertion helpers.

findings.title: test-update-check.sh:76 uses GNU grep \| extension — not POSIX portable
evidence: \\| in basic grep is a GNU extension. POSIX grep does not support it.
On BSD/macOS grep, this causes grep to exit 2 (syntax error), which with set -e aborts the script.
findings.why: The test uses a GNU-specific grep feature without -E flag. While it works on Linux, it introduces a platform dependency that could fail on macOS/BSD. Combined with set -e, a grep exit 2 would silently abort the test.
findings.source: SWE@Google Ch.11 — tests should be portable across developer machines; POSIX compatibility
findings.fix_sketch: Replace with 'grep -E "symlink.*broken|symlink.*missing"' for POSIX extended regex, or use two separate grep calls.

findings.title: test-update-check.sh:66-72 mutates real workspaces/ — not hermetic but acceptable (CI=throwaway, local=gitignored)
evidence: workspaces/ is gitignored — user data, not version-controlled
In CI: runs inside throwaway container/VM — safe
Risk: if test interrupted locally, cleanup (lines 83-86) doesn't run — orphaned dir+symlink
findings.why: The test mutates the production workspaces/ directory rather than a temp dir. In CI this is harmless (throwaway environment). Locally, workspaces/ is gitignored user data, so impact is limited to leaving stale dir+symlink on interrupt. Minor hermeticy violation, not destructive. Demoted from P1 per stakeholder review.
findings.source: SWE@Google Ch.11 — hermeticy is ideal but context-appropriate
findings.fix_sketch: Optionally add trap EXIT for guaranteed cleanup on interrupt: 'trap "rm -rf \"\$MOCK_WS_DIR/default-project\" \"\$MOCK_WS_DIR/CURRENT_WORKSPACE\"" EXIT' before the if-block.

findings.title: migrations/_template.sh excluded from bash -n — could harbor syntax errors
evidence: scripts/test/test-migrations.sh — template.sh is explicitly filtered out of the bash -n loop
migrations/_template.sh is the scaffold for new migrations — bugs here propagate to all future migrations
findings.why: _template.sh is excluded from bash -n because it's a template (not a real migration). But it's still a bash script — if it has syntax errors, every migration created from it will inherit those errors. Testing the template prevents cascading bugs.
findings.source: SWE@Google Ch.12 Principle 1 — strive for unchanging tests; prevent cascading failures
findings.fix_sketch: Add bash -n check for _template.sh. Add a test that verifies the template contains required markers (MIGRATION_NAME, LOG_FILE, etc.). Apply bash -n after placeholder substitution.

findings.title: No test coverage gaps mapped in MEMORY.md or navigation.md
evidence: No test manifest or coverage map exists
The relationship between production scripts and their tests is discoverable only by grep
New contributors don't know which scripts are tested and which aren't
findings.why: Without a coverage map, test gaps are invisible. The team can't answer 'which scripts are tested?' without grep. This makes it hard to prioritize test additions and evaluate coverage completeness.
findings.source: SWE@Google Ch.11 — documentation of coverage is as important as coverage itself
findings.fix_sketch: Add a TEST-COVERAGE.md or update MEMORY.md with a production-script → test-script map. Auto-generate from a script that greps for file references.
